$comment: Bare function-to-pointer decay (C 6.3.2.1p4): a function NAME (no `&`) assigned/initialized/passed where a function pointer is expected decays to the function's address. Closes the `fp = add` regression of D-SEMANTIC-ASSIGN-STMT-ASSIGNABILITY-BYPASS (901fe89) — the new assign-stmt assignability check rejected bare decay (S0003) while `fp = &add` passed. Fixed by the function-to-pointer decay arm in isAssignable (the SHARED chokepoint), so assignment + initializer + call-argument all type-check at once. RED-ON-DISABLE: revert the isAssignable fn-decay arm and the bare-name uses fail to compile -> the example no longer BUILDS (compile failure, not a wrong exit). The whole existing fnptr corpus uses `&fn`, so this bare form is the coverage the suite lacked. Exit: fp(10,5)=15 + gp(3,4)=12 + apply(add,1,10,5)=15 = 42. Fold-resistant: apply is self-recursive (never inlined), the fn-ptr stays an indirect Arg call in baseline AND release. arm64 under qemu; macho on the macos-latest leg.
